source_jobs.title: GTM Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ZYPHRA IS AN ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE COMPANY BASED IN SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA.




THE ROLE:

As a GTM Engineer, you will be a core builder on Zyphra's GTM team, responsible for designing, deploying, and continuously improving the AI-driven systems that power our go-to-market engine. This is not a traditional sales, sales ops or RevOps role. You will bring direct GTM domain expertise and deep, hands-on proficiency with modern AI tools and you'll use both to build infrastructure that creates lasting commercial leverage.



The best candidates for this role have done the hard work of GTM: outbound strategy, customer segmentation, messaging, business development and have also built tangible applications with AI tools. You know where the manual work hides, and you know how to engineer it away.




YOU’LL WORK ACROSS:

 - Designing and deploying end-to-end AI-powered GTM workflows spanning prospecting, outreach, enrichment, qualification, and pipeline management

 - Building and owning the GTM tech stack with tools like Clay, n8n, HubSpot, and custom integrations, and architecting the systems that connect them

 - Developing and iterating customer segmentation models, ICP scoring frameworks, and signal-based targeting systems using enrichment data and AI

 - Rapid-prototyping new GTM motions, running structured experiments with real pipeline data, and scaling what works

 - Using Claude Code, Cursor, and other AI coding tools to build custom automations, scripts, and lightweight applications that accelerate GTM output

 - Partnering with sales, marketing, and business development to translate business needs into automated systems and measuring the revenue impact

 - Identifying new areas where AI tools can be applied to GTM functions, staying ahead of what's possible and experimenting before it becomes standard practice

 - Developing communications processes, messaging frameworks, outreach sequences, and content systems that can be personalized at scale through AI




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR / REQUIREMENTS:

 - 3–5 years of hands-on experience in B2B go-to-market roles: business development, sales operations, growth, or revenue operations, with direct exposure to customer segmentation, targeting, outbound strategy, and pipeline generation

 - Deep, working proficiency with AI coding tools including Claude Code and Cursor/Copilot, you build with these tools daily, not occasionally

 - Experience building and automating workflows with tools like n8n, Zapier, Make, or similar platforms, with the ability to design and deploy complex multi-step automations

 - Hands-on experience with Clay, Apollo, or equivalent data enrichment and prospecting platforms as a core part of your GTM toolset

 - A systems thinker who designs for scale, you see manual GTM work as an engineering problem and default to automating it

 - Excellent communication and collaboration skills; the ability to work across sales, marketing, product, and research teams to understand needs and ship solutions

 - Demonstrated ability to operate in fast-moving, ambiguous environments where the playbook doesn't exist yet




QUALIFICATIONS / ADDITIONAL SKILLS:

 - Experience with CRM platforms (HubSpot, Salesforce) including building custom workflows, lifecycle stages, and reporting infrastructure

 - Comfort writing Python or SQL to build custom integrations, data pipelines, or lightweight internal tools when off-the-shelf solutions aren't enough

 - Familiarity with LLM prompting techniques and orchestration frameworks as applied to GTM use cases: process optimization, outreach personalization, research automation, scoring, summarization

 - Experience measuring and reporting on GTM automation ROI: pipeline generated, time saved, conversion improvements, CAC reduction

 - Comfort designing and testing messaging frameworks at the ICP, vertical, or persona level and using AI to personalize at scale

 - Prior experience at an early-stage or high-growth AI company, or strong

source_jobs.title: AI Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ZYPHRA IS AN ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE COMPANY BASED IN SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA.




THE ROLE:

As a AI Engineer, you will be a core contributor to Zyphra’s Agentic Systems and Interaction projects. You will be at the forefront of building a next-generation desktop and browser-based agent that can autonomously navigate the web, interact with filesystems, and complete complex user tasks. This role spans frontend interfaces, secure sandboxing environments, large-scale document search and retrieval, and language/vision model integration.




YOU’LL WORK ACROSS:

 - Design and implementation of an agentic system capable of interacting with browsers, operating systems, and enterprise filesystems

 - Building search and retrieval pipelines across large-scale structured and unstructured data

 - Integrating LLMs, vision models, reinforcement learning, and scaffolding frameworks for autonomous, multi-step decision-making

 - Engineering secure virtualized runtimes and backend services for agent execution

 - What matters most is your drive to build production-grade ML systems that push the boundary of what software agents can do

 - We value velocity and curiosity, especially in fast-moving and ambiguous environments




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR / REQUIREMENTS:

 - Proficiency in Python and a deep understanding of building and debugging complex ML-driven applications

 - Experience working with desktop operating systems (Windows and macOS), including APIs for screen reading, file interaction, and accessibility frameworks

 - Experience developing browser extensions or automation tools with fine-grained control over the browser (mouse, tabs, DOM)

 - Understanding of LLMs, prompting techniques, and orchestration frameworks for multi-step reasoning

 - Ability to work across the full ML stack, from model integration to serving infrastructure

 - Experience designing or working with secure and virtualized execution environments

 - Excellent communication and collaboration skills across product, research, and engineering teams




QUALIFICATIONS / ADDITIONAL SKILLS:

 - Experience building or integrating retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems

 - Experience working with enterprise security and compliance frameworks (e.g., SOC 2)

 - Familiarity with vector databases and large-scale document indexing

 - Knowledge of web automation tools and headless browser environments (e.g, Puppeteer, Playwright)

 - Understanding of sandboxed or containerized compute environments with strict access controls

 - Comfort designing user-facing agentic workflows and reasoning systems that span multiple modalities (text, vision, actions)

 - Experience using and fine-tuning models for screen reading, OCR, or UI understanding

 - Background in HCI or interest in building intuitive agent interfaces that extend human capabilities




WHY WORK AT ZYPHRA:

 - Our research methodology is to make grounded, methodical steps toward ambitious goals. Both deep research and engineering excellence are equally valued

 - We strongly value new and crazy ideas and are very willing to bet big on new ideas

 - We move as quickly as we can; we aim to minimize the bar to impact as low as possible

 - We all enjoy what we do and love discussing AI




BENEFITS AND PERKS:

 - Comprehensive medical, dental, vision, and FSA plans

 - Competitive compensation and 401(k) plan

 - Relocation and immigration support on a case-by-case basis

 - In-office snacks and meals provided

 - Unlimited PTO and company holidays

 - In-person team in San Francisco with a collaborative, high-energy environment

source_jobs.title: Data Engineer - Multimodal Systems
source_jobs.description:
ZYPHRA IS AN ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE COMPANY BASED IN SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA.




THE ROLE:

As a Data Engineer - Multimodal Systems, you will be a core contributor to creating, collecting, and improving Zyphra’s datasets and data pipelines across a variety of modalities. Your work will intersect with almost every team at Zyphra. You will be involved in collecting large-scale datasets and implementing and optimizing highly parallel data pipelines.




YOU’LL WORK ACROSS:

 - Large-scale data collection across a variety of modalities (text, audio, image)

 - Designing and working with highly efficient, parallelized data processing pipelines across modalities

 - Designing and running rigorous experimental ablations to demonstrate the impact of new data improvements




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR / REQUIREMENTS:

 - Strong implementation and prototyping ability

 - Can take an idea from conception to experimentation quickly

 - The ability to work well with others in a high-paced research setting

 - Can rapidly learn new fields and are excited to implement new ideas

 - Excellent communication and collaboration skills, and can work effectively on both research and engineering implementation at scale.




QUALIFICATIONS / ADDITIONAL SKILLS:

 - Experience collecting, handling, and processing large datasets

 - Experience with parallel Python programming frameworks such as Dask

 - Understanding of the state-of-the-art in dataset curation across modalities

 - A generally meticulous nature and a strong interest in actually looking at data and sanity checking things

 - Strong grasp of proper experimental methodology for running rigorous ablations and other hypothesis testing

 - Understanding of and interest in large-scale, highly parallel data processing pipelines.

 - Proficiency with PyTorch and Python.

 - Experience contributing to large pre-existing codebases and rapidly getting up to speed.

 - Previously published machine learning research in well-respected venues.

 - Postgraduate degree in a scientific subject (Computer Science, EE/EECS, Mathematics, Physics, Machine Learning)




WHY WORK AT ZYPHRA:

 - Our research methodology is grounded in methodical, step-by-step approaches to ambitious goals. Both deep research and engineering excellence are equally valued

 - We strongly value new and crazy ideas and are very willing to bet big on new ideas

 - We move as quickly as we can; we aim to minimize the bar to impact as low as possible

 - We all enjoy what we do and love discussing AI




BENEFITS AND PERKS:

 - Comprehensive medical, dental, vision, and FSA plans

 - Competitive compensation and 401(k) plan

 - Relocation and immigration support on a case-by-case basis

 - In-office snacks and meals provided

 - Unlimited PTO and company holidays

 - In-person team in San Francisco with a collaborative, high-energy environment

source_jobs.title: Research Engineer - Audio & Speech Models

ZYPHRA IS AN ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE COMPANY BASED IN SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA.

As a Research Engineer - Audio & Speech Models, you will be a core contributor on Zyphra’s Audio Team, building the next generation of open-source autoencoders, ASR, TTS, SSL, and speech-to-speech models.  - Previously published machine learning research in well-respected venues

 - Postgraduate degree in a scientific subject (Computer Science, EE/EECS, Mathematics, Physics, Machine Learning)




WHY WORK AT ZYPHRA:

 - Our research methodology is grounded in methodical, step-by-step approaches to ambitious goals. Both deep research and engineering excellence are equally valued

source_jobs.title: Developer Relations Lead
source_jobs.description:
ZYPHRA IS AN ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE COMPANY BASED IN SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA.




THE ROLE:

As a Developer Relations Lead, you will own the developer community that forms around Zyphra’s open-source models and Zyphra’s AI agent, Maia from the ground up. You will be the primary face of Zyphra to the global developer community, building and scaling our presence on GitHub, Discord, X, and at the industry events that matter most. This is not a content production role. You are a technically credible practitioner who is also a natural community builder: someone developers trust, follow, and build with.



There is an early community already forming around Zyphra’s models. Your job is to find it, accelerate it, structure it, and turn it into one of the most active open-source AI communities in the field. You will be a critical member of the GTM team and work in close collaboration with Zyphra’s research, engineering, and product teams.




YOU’LL WORK ACROSS:

 - Owning the development, expansion, and moderation of Zyphra’s developer community end-to-end across all public channels including Discord, GitHub, and Hugging Face while also growing our developer brand across X, LinkedIn, and other technical channels

 - Representing Zyphra at AI conferences, developer summits, open-source events, academic forums, and community meetups: speaking, demoing, and building relationships that expand our reach and credibility in the developer ecosystem

 - Driving GitHub adoption metrics across Zyphra’s open-source model repositories: stars, forks, issues, pull requests, and active contributor velocity as direct measures of ecosystem health

 - Acting as the primary feedback channel between Zyphra’s developer community and the research and product teams, synthesizing what developers are building, where they are getting stuck, and what would unlock the next wave of adoption across modalities

 - Building and executing a community growth strategy that converts each model release into sustained developer engagement: launch moments, contributor programs, ambassador networks, and structured onboarding for new community members

 - Driving API consumption and model usage, helping developers understand what Zyphra’s models and agent can do, where they are differentiated, and how to get started quickly and successfully

 - Creating and curating technical content that serves the community: integration guides, benchmark comparisons, use-case demos, deployment walkthroughs, and example projects that showcase what developers can build on Zyphra’s open foundation models and inference infrastructure

 - Partnering with the GTM team on model launch strategy, open-source announcements, and developer-facing go-to-market motions that maximize community impact at each release




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR / REQUIREMENTS:

 - A genuine community participant, you have built with open-source AI models, understand how developers think about evaluating and integrating foundation models, and can speak credibly in technical conversations with researchers and engineers

 - 3–6 years of experience in developer relations, developer advocacy, open-source community management, or a closely related technical role at an AI, ML, or developer tools company

 - Demonstrated track record of building or meaningfully growing a technical developer community measured in GitHub engagement, Discord or Slack membership, social following, or conference presence

 - Comfort and credibility on stage: you have spoken at developer conferences, hosted workshops, or led technical demos in front of audiences of engineers

 - Strong technical foundation in AI and machine learning, you understand model architecture, inference, fine-tuning, and deployment well enough to engage authentically with the developers building on Zyphra’s models and Maia

 - A natural communicator who can explain highly technical concepts clearly across formats: live presentations, written posts, short-form social content, a

source_jobs.title: Research Engineer - Language Model Pre-Training
source_jobs.description:
ZYPHRA IS AN ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE COMPANY BASED IN SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA.




THE ROLE:

As a Research Engineer - Language Model Pre-Training, you'll shape our language model roadmap through end-to-end pretraining development. You will work extremely closely with our pretraining team, who will integrate your insights into our next-generation models.




YOU'LL WORK ACROSS:

 - Performance optimization of our pretraining stack

 - Dataset collection, processing, and evaluation

WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR / REQUIREMENTS:

 - Can rapidly learn new fields and are excited to implement new ideas

 - Strong ability to dive into large pre-existing codebases and rapidly get up to speed

source_jobs.title: Research Engineer - Agency and Reasoning
source_jobs.description:
ZYPHRA IS AN ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE COMPANY BASED IN SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA.




THE ROLE:

As a Research Engineer - Agency and Reasoning, you will be a core contributor to Zyphra’s Agency and Reasoning Team. You will be involved with performing novel research in reinforcement learning, post-training, and human preference learning, and applying your ideas at scale to our next generation of language models.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR / REQUIREMENTS:

 - Strong research taste and intuition

 - The ability to work through a research project from conception to execution to write-up

 - Strong implementation and prototyping skillset

 - A researcher who can take an idea from conception to experimentation extremely quickly

 - The ability to work well and cooperate with others in a high-paced research setting

 - Curiosity, interest, and joy in understanding intelligence.




QUALIFICATIONS / ADDITIONAL SKILLS:

 - Experience and aptitude with reinforcement learning, either in the context of language model reasoning or more classical RL tasks

 - Experience with language-model-supervised fine-tuning and preference-learning methods, such as DPO and simPO.

 - Experience with context-length extension methods

 - A good intuitive ability to understand model behaviors and correct them through iterative fine-tuning

 - Interest in grappling in detail with data and spending significant time involved in data engineering and synthetic data generation

 - Postgraduate degree in a scientific subject (Computer Science, EE/EECS, Mathematics, Physics)

WHY WORK AT ZYPHRA:

source_jobs.title: Full-Stack Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ZYPHRA IS AN ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE COMPANY BASED IN SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA.




THE ROLE:

As a Full-Stack Software Engineer, you will be a core contributor to Zyphra’s Agentic Systems and Interaction projects. You will be at the forefront of building a next-generation desktop and browser-based agent (end-to-end) that can autonomously navigate the web, interact with filesystems, and complete complex user tasks. This role spans agentic orchestration, frontend interfaces, secure sandboxing environments, large-scale document search and retrieval, and language/vision model integration.




YOU’LL WORK ACROSS:

 - Design and implementation of different agentic system designs capable of interacting with browsers, operating systems, enterprise filesystems, collaboration tools, etc.

 - Building search and retrieval pipelines across large-scale structured and unstructured data

 - Build the backend layer for RAG systems and other information context management systems required for agents to operate

 - Integrating LLMs, vision models, reinforcement learning, and scaffolding frameworks for autonomous, multi-step decision-making

 - What matters most is your drive to build production-grade software

 - We value velocity and curiosity, especially in fast-moving and ambiguous environments




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR / REQUIREMENTS:

 - Proficiency in Python and a deep understanding of building and debugging complex end-to-end applications

 - Experience working with SaaS environment and production workloads, web interfaces, databases, search engines, and queuing systems

 - Experience developing browser extensions or automation tools with fine-grained control over the browser (mouse, tabs, DOM)

 - Understanding of LLMs, prompting techniques, and orchestration frameworks for multi-step reasoning

 - Ability to work across the whole stack from web interfaces to control plane and data infrastructure like queuing systems, databases, and search indexes

 - Experience designing or working with secure and virtualized execution environments

 - Excellent communication and collaboration skills across product, research, and engineering teams

 - Experience using and fine-tuning models for screen reading, OCR, or UI understanding

 - Machine Learning experience as a double bonus

source_jobs.title: Principal Solutions Architect
source_jobs.description:
ZYPHRA IS AN ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE COMPANY BASED IN SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA.




THE ROLE:

As a Principal Solutions Architect, you will serve as the primary technical authority in all partner and customer-facing engagements across Zyphra’s full model portfolio and AI agent. You will articulate the technical value and differentiation of each of Zyphra’s AI models and agentic systems, guide partners and customers from initial evaluation through successful integration, and act as the face of our research. This is not a support role. You will own the full technical GTM motion: building the narratives, converting strategic relationships, and supporting commercial outcomes.



You will also be a critical voice feeding partner and customer intelligence back into Zyphra’s research and product roadmap. The best candidates for this role think like researchers, communicate like an industry expert and have the credibility to operate at the highest levels of technical and executive conversation.




YOU’LL WORK ACROSS:

 - Serving as the primary technical advisor to strategic partners and customers across Zyphra’s full AI model portfolio: including language, vision, audio, brain computer interface and agentic systems from initial discovery through deployment and expansion

 - Developing and delivering compelling technical narratives, demos, and evaluation frameworks that articulate the differentiation of each Zyphra model to audiences ranging from research teams and engineers to executives and business decision-makers

 - Leading technical discovery conversations with partners and customers to deeply understand their needs, use cases, and integration requirements, and translating those needs into actionable steps

 - Driving strategic partnership development and customer conversion, owning the technical relationship through the full commercial lifecycle

 - Synthesizing partner and customer intelligence into emerging use cases, unmet needs, deployment patterns, and competitive signals and delivering structured feedback that directly influences Zyphra’s research and product priorities

 - Building and maintaining a library of reusable technical assets: model evaluation frameworks, fine-tuning guides, deployment architectures, API integration playbooks, and use-case demos that accelerate how partners and customers adopt Zyphra's models

 - Representing Zyphra at industry conferences, technical workshops, academic forums, and partner events to build credibility and expand Zyphra’s technical presence

 - Collaborating closely with the GTM team, researchers, and engineers to align external technical positioning with internal capability and roadmap




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR / REQUIREMENTS:

 - Deep technical fluency across one or more of Zyphra’s core domains: large language models and model architecture, multimodal AI, speech and audio synthesis, brain-computer interfaces and neural signal processing, or agentic AI systems

 - A PhD in machine learning, computer science, neuroscience or a closely related field is strongly preferred; exceptional candidates with a Master’s degree and demonstrated research depth will be considered

 - Demonstrated ability to communicate highly technical concepts clearly and compellingly to diverse audiences from key engineers to executives without sacrificing accuracy or depth

 - Prior experience in a customer-facing, partner-facing, or commercially-oriented technical role, solutions architecture, applied research, developer relations, technical business development, or equivalent

 - Strong instincts for identifying where AI technology creates differentiated commercial value, and the ability to build a persuasive case around that value in competitive contexts

 - Comfort operating in ambiguous, fast-moving environments where the product is evolving, the market is forming, and the playbook is still being written

 - Excellent collaboration skills across research, engineering, and GTM teams




QUALIFICATION

source_jobs.title: Research Engineer - Brain Computer Interface Models
source_jobs.description:
ZYPHRA IS AN ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE COMPANY BASED IN SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA.

source_jobs.title: Lead Frontend Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ZYPHRA IS AN ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE COMPANY BASED IN SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA.




THE ROLE:

As a Lead Frontend Engineer, you’ll lead the design and development of the user-facing side of our agentic system, shaping how users interact with AI across their desktop, browser, and filesystem. You’ll work across interface design, component libraries, and frontend infrastructure to craft seamless, accessible, and intuitive user experiences. You’ll work closely with engineers, ML researchers, and backend teams to bring intelligent agents to life in a way that’s beautifully simple and deeply useful.




YOU’LL WORK ACROSS:

 - Design and development of browser-based and desktop interfaces

 - Building and maintaining component libraries and styling systems

 - Researching and refining user flows based on real-world usage and feedback

 - Prototyping and implementing UI features in collaboration with ML, backend, and systems teams

 - Making a complex AI product feel simple, accessible, and empowering for a wide range of users




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR / REQUIREMENTS:

 - Strong frontend engineering skills with fluency in HTML, CSS, and JavaScript/TypeScript

 - Experience with modern frontend frameworks such as React

 - Demonstrated attention to UI detail and usability, with a user-first mindset

 - Ability to prototype and iterate quickly, both in code and with design tools like Figma

 - Experience designing and developing responsive, accessible, and high-performance interfaces

 - Strong intuition for HCI principles, accessibility, and visual clarity

 - Excellent collaboration and communication skills—especially with engineers, designers, and ML researchers




QUALIFICATIONS / ADDITIONAL SKILLS:

 - Experience designing or building browser extensions or desktop applications (e.g., Electron, Tauri)

 - Experience with mobile platforms and responsive design (not required but helpful)

 - Prior work on products involving complex workflows, automation, or agentic behavior

 - Interest or experience in backend development, APIs, or AI systems to better interface with the full stack

 - Familiarity with accessibility standards and inclusive design best practices

 - A track record of launching polished and user-loved products

 - Passion for systems-level thinking: building reusable design systems, libraries, and scalable patterns

 - A clear, opinionated eye for elegant design and user delight




WHY WORK AT ZYPHRA:

 - Our research methodology is grounded in methodical, step-by-step approaches to ambitious goals. Both deep research and engineering excellence are equally valued

 - We strongly value new and crazy ideas and are very willing to bet big on new ideas

 - We move as quickly as we can; we aim to minimize the bar to impact as low as possible

 - Unlimited PTO and company holidays

 - In-person team in San Francisco with a collaborative, high-energy environment

source_jobs.title: Platform Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ZYPHRA IS AN ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE COMPANY BASED IN SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA.




THE ROLE:

As a Platform Engineer, you’ll be responsible for designing and maintaining the systems that keep Zyphra’s infrastructure robust, observable, secure, and scalable. Your work will be essential to ensuring the reliability and reproducibility of ML workloads, the safety and control of deployments, and the long-term maintainability of our compute environments.




YOU’LL WORK ACROSS:

 - Building and improving observability systems (monitoring, logging, alerting)

 - Managing Infrastructure as a Service across the stack along with CI/CD in close partnership with engineering teams

 - Designing resilient build and deployment systems across research and production environments

 - Implementing secure release processes with strong auditability and rollback support

 - Collaborating closely with ML engineers, DevOps, and infra teams to improve system reliability and performance

 - Leading incident response, root-cause analysis, and postmortems with a focus on learning and prevention

 - This role is ideal for someone who loves building systems that make other teams faster, safer, and more productive




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR / REQUIREMENTS:

 - Experience in high-performance compute environments, such as ML clusters or GPU farms as well as hyperscaler cloud environments (i.e. AWS, GCP, etc.)

 - Background in infrastructure as code (i.e., Terraform, Ansible, etc.)

 - Familiarity with containers (i.e., Docker, Apptainer) and their integration with scheduling systems (i.e., Kubernetes, Slurm)

 - Familiarity with software release engineering for ML/AI systems is a plus

 - Experience managing run-books, DRP, change management, and general fault tolerance

 - Experience with deployment strategies at scale

 - Experience designing reliable environments for experimental workloads and reproducible runs

 - Knowledge of compliance and audit standards in deployment and system security

 - Experience with load testing, fault injection, and chaos engineering to harden systems under stress

 - Passion for building tooling that makes infrastructure invisible and reliable for end users




QUALIFICATIONS / ADDITIONAL SKILLS:

 - Experience with infrastructure as code (e.g., Ansible, Terraform)

 - Prior work supporting ML/AI infrastructure, including GPU management and workload optimization

 - Exposure to backend development for ML model serving (i.e., vLLM, Ray, SGLang, Triton)




WHY WORK AT ZYPHRA:

 - Our research methodology is grounded in methodical, step-by-step approaches to ambitious goals. Both deep research and engineering excellence are equally valued

 - We strongly value new and crazy ideas and are very willing to bet big on new ideas

 - We move as quickly as we can; we aim to minimize the bar to impact as low as possible

 - We all enjoy what we do and love discussing AI




BENEFITS AND PERKS:

 - Comprehensive medical, dental, vision, and FSA plans

 - Competitive compensation and 401(k) plan

 - Relocation and immigration support on a case-by-case basis

 - In-office snacks and meals provided

 - Unlimited PTO and company holidays

 - In-person team in San Francisco with a collaborative, high-energy environment

source_jobs.title: Research Engineer - AI Performance & Kernel Optimization
source_jobs.description:
ZYPHRA IS AN ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE COMPANY BASED IN SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA.




THE ROLE:

As a Research Engineer - AI Performance & Kernel Optimization, you will improve and optimize the performance of our large-scale language model training and inference stacks. You will work closely with our pretraining and inference teams to identify bottlenecks, design and implement highly optimized kernels, and push the limits of throughput, latency, and hardware utilization across a range of accelerator platforms. This role is suited for someone who enjoys deep systems work, cares about performance at every level of the stack, and is excited to translate low-level optimizations into meaningful gains for frontier-scale AI systems.




YOU’LL WORK ACROSS:

 - Kernel development and optimization for large-scale ML workloads, using any level of the stack from PTX/assembly to CUDA, HIP, Triton, or other GPU DSLs

 - Performance tuning for training and inference stacks across GPUs and other accelerators

 - Profiling and eliminating bottlenecks in memory movement, communication, scheduling, and compute utilization

 - Optimizing distributed training and inference systems for large MoE models, including large-scale model parallelism

 - Portability and optimization across non-NVIDIA hardware, with special interest in AMD hardware such as the MI300x and MI355x

 - Collaboration with research and infrastructure teams to turn systems improvements into real-world model training and inference gains




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR / REQUIREMENTS:

 - Strong engineering aptitude for building reliable, high-performance systems

 - Excellent low-level performance intuition and the ability to reason about hardware-software interactions

 - Are excited to rapidly learn new systems, tools, and hardware environments

 - Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively across research and engineering teams

 - Enjoy diving deep into the weeds and hunting down the last 10–20% of performance




QUALIFICATIONS / ADDITIONAL SKILLS:

 - Experience writing highly performant GPU kernels at any level of abstraction–PTX, CUDA, HIP, Triton, or other kernel DSLs

 - Experience optimizing ML workloads for large-scale training, ideally in language model pretraining or inference environments

 - Experience with non-NVIDIA accelerator hardware, such as AMD, AWS Trainium, Google TPU, Qualcomm, ARM, Intel, and custom ASICs

 - Strong understanding of distributed training systems and parallelism schemes, including data parallelism, tensor/model parallelism, pipeline parallelism, sharding, and communication/computation overlap

 - Experience with performance engineering in other demanding parallel computing environments such as HPC, quantitative finance, scientific computing, graphics, compilers, or numerical simulation

 - Strong systems intuition around memory hierarchy, bandwidth constraints, kernel fusion, launch overhead, communication overhead, and hardware utilization

 - Experience using profiling and debugging tools to drive performance improvements

 - Familiarity with infrastructure underlying large-scale training and inference, including collective communication libraries, and runtime performance analysis

 - Background in a highly technical field such as physics, mathematics, theoretical computer science, computer science, or electrical engineering

 - Any HPC experience is a strong plus




WHY WORK AT ZYPHRA:

 - Our research methodology is grounded in methodical, step-by-step approaches to ambitious goals. Both deep research and engineering excellence are equally valued

 - We strongly value new and crazy ideas and are very willing to bet big on new ideas

 - We move as quickly as we can; we aim to minimize the bar to impact as low as possible

 - We all enjoy what we do and love discussing AI




BENEFITS AND PERKS:

 - Comprehensive medical, dental, vision, and FSA plans

 - Competitive compensation and 401(k) plan

 -

source_jobs.title: Research Engineer - Model Architectures
source_jobs.description:
ZYPHRA IS AN ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE COMPANY BASED IN SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA.




THE ROLE:

As a Research Engineer - Model Architectures, you will be a core contributor to Zyphra’s AI Architecture Research Team. This will involve designing and rigorously testing novel model architectures and training methodologies, with a focus on improving core modeling capabilities (e.g., loss per flop or loss per parameter) and addressing fundamental bottlenecks in contemporary models. You will also work extremely closely with our pre-training team, who will integrate your insights into our next-generation models.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR / REQUIREMENTS:

 - Strong research taste and intuition

 - The ability to work through a research project from conception to execution to write-up

 - Strong implementation and prototyping ability can take an idea from conception to experimentation extremely quickly

 - The ability to work well and cooperate with others in a high-paced research setting

 - Curiosity, interest, and joy in understanding intelligence.




QUALIFICATIONS / ADDITIONAL SKILLS:

 - Previous experience with long-term memory, RAG/retrieval systems, dynamic/adaptive computation, and alternative approaches to credit assignment

 - Experience with reinforcement learning, control theory, and signal processing

 - Generally, a joy in inventing and seriously assessing ‘crazy’ ideas, and the ability to have a unique perspective on things

 - Understanding of modern training pipelines and the hardware requirements to design efficient architectures for GPU hardware

 - Strong grasp of proper experimental methodology for running rigorous ablations and other hypothesis testing

 - High proficiency with PyTorch and Python.

 - Strong ability to jump into large pre-existing codebases and rapidly get up to speed and become productive

 - Previously published machine learning research in well-respected venues

 - Postgraduate degree in a scientific subject (Computer Science, EE/EECS, Math, Physics)




WHY WORK AT ZYPHRA:

 - Our research methodology is to make grounded, methodical steps toward ambitious goals. Both deep research and engineering excellence are equally valued

 - We strongly value new and crazy ideas and are very willing to bet big on new ideas

 - We move as quickly as we can; we aim to minimize the bar to impact as low as possible

 - We all enjoy what we do and love discussing AI




BENEFITS AND PERKS:

 - Comprehensive medical, dental, vision, and FSA plans

 - Competitive compensation and 401(k) plan

 - Relocation and immigration support on a case-by-case basis

 - In-office snacks and meals provided

 - Unlimited PTO and company holidays

 - In-person team in San Francisco with a collaborative, high-energy environment

notes: Zyphra is an AI company with a strong focus on engineering and research roles.
